description: Cross-app token management foundation: Ensure packages/api-client has a proper token management system that all apps can use - set on login, auto-refresh, clear on logout. Add NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL env var pattern documentation and examples across all three apps.
steps: Step 1: In packages/api-client/src/core/http-client.ts, add auto-refresh token logic. Add a setRefreshToken(token) method, an onTokenRefresh callback, and an onAuthError callback. When a 401 is received and a refresh token is available, attempt to refresh via POST /api/auth/refresh before retrying the original request. If refresh fails, call onAuthError.

Step 2: Add a new file packages/api-client/src/core/token-manager.ts that provides a singleton TokenManager class. It stores access/refresh tokens in memory (with optional localStorage persistence for browser), exposes setTokens(access, refresh), getAccessToken(), clearTokens(), isAuthenticated(), and an onTokenChange event. This is the single source of truth for auth state.

Step 3: Update packages/api-client/src/index.ts to export the TokenManager and connect it to HttpClient. Add a factory function createApiClient(config) that wires TokenManager to HttpClient automatically.

Step 4: Verify that the existing auth patterns in each app (apps/quantchat/src/lib/auth.ts reads from localStorage, apps/quantai/src/lib/auth.ts presumably does the same) are compatible with the TokenManager. Do not break existing patterns - make TokenManager work alongside them by reading from the same localStorage keys.
Step 5: Create .env.local.example files (if not already done in FEAT-001/002/003) for each app documenting all required env vars.
acceptance_criteria: packages/api-client/src/core/token-manager.ts exports TokenManager class with setTokens, getAccessToken, clearTokens, isAuthenticated
HttpClient can auto-refresh tokens on 401 when configured with a refresh endpoint
